description: A square designed graphic on a pale gray-blue background, styled to resemble a simplified browser or app window: a rounded-rectangle outline frame in medium blue with three short dashes (like window controls / a menu) in the upper left and the DHS seal plus navy 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in the upper right. Inside the frame, large navy text reads 'There have been almost' with '47,000' rendered in bold red and 'submissions as of April 29' completing the line. Dominating the lower half is a large stylized 'ARR' logo: the letters 'ARR' in bold blue with a swooping red-and-blue ribbon/swoosh curving around the first letter, above the words 'Alien Registration Requirement' (with 'Requirement' in bold). The style is flat, modern, infographic. Messaging technique: a participation/compliance statistic promoting the new Alien Registration Requirement program and implying broad uptake; paired with the tweet's warning that failure to comply may bring fines or removal. Target audience: noncitizens required to register and the public, framing the registration mandate as active and being followed.
notable_text: There have been almost 47,000 submissions as of April 29 / ARR Alien Registration Requirement
